title: 🎙️ Science, Wonder & the Natural World
description: Today we look at scientific discovery and the natural world — from ecology and wildlife research to astronomy and deep-sea biology. We're grounding these stories in the landscapes and living systems of the Cariboo: what salmon runs, boreal forest research, or species-at-risk studies tell us about the wild places we live beside. We're after stories that inspire curiosity and wonder about how the world works. - Daily themed article selection for podcast generation

_podcast.theme_description: Today we look at scientific discovery and the natural world — from ecology and wildlife research to astronomy and deep-sea biology. We're grounding these stories in the landscapes and living systems of the Cariboo: what salmon runs, boreal forest research, or species-at-risk studies tell us about the wild places we live beside. We're after stories that inspire curiosity and wonder about how the world works.
_podcast.theme_scoring_prompt: Rate how well this article fits the theme 'Science, Wonder & the Natural World' — scientific discovery, ecology, natural phenomena, and the curiosity-driven study of living systems. Strong fits include: wildlife biology and species research, ecology studies and biodiversity findings, watershed and salmon research relevant to BC, astronomy and space science, new species discoveries, citizen science projects, natural history stories, wildfire science and forest ecology, climate science findings (not just policy), geology, evolution, and deep-time Earth stories, and any scientific research grounded in BC or Cariboo landscapes and ecosystems. Prefer articles that convey genuine curiosity and discovery — the 'how' and 'why' of the natural world. Score 70–90 for: prehistoric life and palaeontology (ancient oceans, Cretaceous ecology, deep-time species), new species discoveries in ocean or remote environments, deep-sea biology, evolutionary biology findings, natural history stories with a strong sense of wonder, and field research grounded in BC ecosystems. Penalise (score 0–30): consumer health and pharmaceutical research without ecology or natural-world context; pure policy and advocacy news; technology business stories without a science or ecology angle; human interest and viral news without scientific substance; geopolitical and crime news. Score 0-100 where 100 is a perfect thematic match and 0 is completely unrelated.

## BCWS responding to 13 out of control wildfires throughout Cariboo Fire Centre Published 12:38 pm Sunday, July 19, 2026 **By Patrick Davies** 1/2 The French Bar Creek wildfire on Saturday, July 18\. items.title: [ScienceDaily] A tiny universe in a bottle reveals clues to the origins of life
items.content_html:
🔭 ✏️

Researchers have created cosmic dust from scratch by recreating space-like conditions inside glass tubes. The dust contains complex carbon-rich molecules built from elements essential to life and produces infrared signals similar to real material found in space. By studying these laboratory samples, scientists can explore how organic chemistry unfolds around stars and how comets, asteroids and meteorites may have carried those ingredients to Earth.
items.summary: Researchers have created cosmic dust from scratch by recreating space-like conditions inside glass tubes. items.title: [Boing Boing] An AWS bug turned a 5-cent bill into $2.5 billion
items.content_html:
🔭 ✏️

On the morning of July 17, a redditor who normally pays Amazon Web Services about five cents a month opened their inbox to a wall of billing alerts and a running tab of $2.5 billion. Their whole setup was a single S3 storage bucket, untouched since 2023 and not even public. — Read the rest

The post An AWS bug turned a 5-cent bill into $2.5 billion appeared first on Boing Boing.

items.title: [New Atlas] Monkeys get freaked out by the ‘uncanny valley’ too
items.content_html:
🔭 ✏️

On the road to constructing flawless simulations of human faces, there’s a zone where things get … weird. Too realistic to be a caricature or cartoon, too fake to be the real deal, these middle-ground representations trigger responses of discomfort in many who encounter them.

Continue Reading

Category: Biology, Science, Refractor: Science & Health

Tags: Psychology, Communications, Future Robot, CGI, Uncanny valley

items._excerpt: On the road to constructing flawless simulations of human faces, there’s a zone where things get … weird. Too realistic to be a caricature or cartoon, too fake to be the real deal, these middle-ground representations trigger responses of discomfort in many who encounter them. Continue Reading Category: Biology , Science , Refractor: Science & Health Tags: Psychology , Communications , Future Robot , CGI , Uncanny valley

items.title: [The Marginalian] The Art of the Sacred Pause and Despair as a Catalyst of Regeneration
items.content_html:
🔭 ✏️

Just as there are transitional times in the life of the world — dark periods of disorientation between two world systems, periods in which humanity loses the ability to comprehend itself and collapses into chaos in order to rebuild itself around a new organizing principle — there are such times in every human life, times when the entire system seems to cave in and curl up into a catatonia of anguish and confusion, difficult yet necessary for our growth. In such times, the most courageous thing we can do is surrender to the process that is the pause, trust the… read article
